libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Disable Ehcache update checks in tests, preferably with System.setProperty("net.sf.ehcache.skipUpdateCheck", "true") at test startup or Configuration.updateCheck(false). If a test starts Bitronix, set bitronix.tm.configuration to a temporary properties file with a unique serverId, bitronix.tm.disableJmx=true, and bitronix.tm.journal=null, then shut Bitronix down and clear the property after the test.

deterministic_setup.reason: ehcache-core 2.5.0 declares JTA as provided and its transaction APIs/classes reference javax.transaction types.
Ehcache's DefaultTransactionManagerLookup can discover and integrate with Bitronix; existing meaningful coverage exercises this transaction-manager path.
Bitronix declares JMS APIs as optional, so they are not reliably present transitively when Bitronix classes are included in JVM/native-image tests.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8877 [Automation] native-image run fails for net.sf.ehcache:ehcache-core:2.5.0; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: ehcache-core also contains optional servlet, Hibernate, Terracotta, and replication integration classes; do not add those dependencies unless tests intentionally cover those integrations.
If update checks are not disabled, CacheManager startup may attempt outbound update-check behavior and leave timer activity, making tests less deterministic.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Ehcache core is usable in-process without Docker, but meaningful coverage of its transaction/XA paths requires provided/optional JTA and Bitronix-related APIs that are not supplied by the normal scaffold.
